Title: Modulation of lignin and anthocyanin homeostasis by GTP cyclohydrolase1 in maize

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins are antioxidants with health benefits; lignin affects cell wall properties, silage digestibility, and biomass utilization.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZmPEBP15 interacts with ZmGCH1 to restrict chalcone synthase activity, thereby helping maintain precursor balance between lignin and anthocyanin biosynthesis; reduced/disrupted ZmPEBP15-associated interaction is linked to increased anthocyanin flux.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS; co-localization in tobacco leaves; BiFC; yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation; luciferase complementation assay; transient co-expression in tobacco followed by CHS activity assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression significantly reduced in bm6_NIL relative to B73
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression significantly reduced in bm6_NIL leaves relative to B73
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field growth; transient expression in tobacco leaves and maize protoplasts
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI SRA: PRJNA1185680); IP-MS; untargeted metabolomics
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Through the ZmGCH1–ZmPEBP15 module, altered precursor allocation is associated with reduced lignin monomers and potential agronomic penalties in some genetic backgrounds.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for tuning lignin/anthocyanin precursor partitioning, though direct deployment requires caution because disruption of the module can impair agronomic performance depending on maize background.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPEBP15 was identified as a ZmGCH1-interacting protein by IP-MS and validated by multiple interaction assays. 2. ZmGCH1 and ZmPEBP15 co-localize and interact in the cytoplasm. 3. Their interaction partially suppresses chalcone synthase activity, stabilizing allocation of p-coumaric acid between lignin and flavonoid/anthocyanin pathways. 4. In bm6, disruption of the interaction is associated with elevated CHS activity and increased anthocyanin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ZmPEBP15 is a core mechanistic partner rather than a catalytic enzyme or transcription factor; no direct mutant of ZmPEBP15 was analyzed in planta for metabolite phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tetrahydrofolate (one-carbon) biosynthesis linked to phenylpropanoid partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: One-carbon/THF synthesis module affecting methyl-donor supply and precursor allocation between lignin and flavonoid/anthocyanin pathways
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point supported; identified as causal bm6 gene affecting THF synthesis and precursor partitioning, but explicit rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first committed step of tetrahydrofolate/pterin biosynthesis by converting GTP to dihydroneopterin triphosphate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor/methyl-donor pathway support and indirectly controls flux toward lignin versus anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lignin contributes to cell wall strength and reduces forage digestibility; anthocyanins are antioxidants beneficial for stress protection and human health.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation/downregulation of ZmGCH1 reduces lignin monomer accumulation and increases anthocyanin/flavonoid accumulation by redirecting shared phenylpropanoid precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fine mapping; candidate gene sequencing; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; enzyme activity assay; lignin and anthocyanin quantification; metabolomics; introgression into elite hybrids; in vitro rumen digestibility assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots, stems, leaves, midribs, tassel, ears and seeds; reduced in bm6_NIL leaves relative to B73
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed across maize tissues; significantly downregulated in bm6_NIL leaves at V8 stage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field conditions in Tai’an and Suihua; V8 stage sampling; introgression into Zhengdan 958 and Xianyu 335 backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: bm6 causes dwarfing in near-isogenic background and can reduce yield-related traits in some maize varieties, especially Xianyu 335; effects are genotype- and environment-dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable target for improving silage digestibility and potentially bioethanol processing by lowering lignin monomers; should be deployed with background selection because yield penalties vary by genotype and region.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fine mapping and expression analysis identified ZmGCH1 (Zm00001d001959) as the bm6 causal gene. 2. bm6 carries two coding SNPs causing F37L and R251H substitutions and reduced ZmGCH1 expression/activity in leaves. 3. ZmGCH1 mutation lowers lignin monomers, increases flavonoids/anthocyanins, and redirects phenylpropanoid flux. 4. Introgression of bm6 improved rumen digestibility in Zhengdan 958, demonstrating breeding value.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Total lignin was not significantly decreased, likely due to compensatory tricin increase despite reduced G and S monomers; ZmGCH1 also affects tetrahydrofolate levels, but methionine/SAM changes were not detected.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter and pCAMBIA1300-based transient expression constructs used in localization/interaction assays
